I have tried a few other stylii (a Targus, a Boxwave, and a couple others), but this one is easily the best. It is as close to writing with pen and paper as a stylus is going to get. Because of the nature of a capacitive screen, there has to be a certain amount of surface area touched for it to register, but this stylus made that as small a problem as possible. All of the other stylii I've used have been thick with a wide foam or rubber mushy tip at the end, making finer details more or less impossible. The small clear disc at the tip makes it much easier to see where exactly the line is being formed. I bought this to use to take handwritten notes on my iPad, and it works brilliantly.There are a few small changes that I would make to make it perfect. There is no clip, so I have to keep it thrown in my bag or in my pocket instead of being able to neatly clip it to my iPad case. Since it's round, it rolls everywhere. Mildly inconvenient.Before I bought this, I read a bunch of reviews on it (I wanted to make sure I knew what I was getting since $20 is kind of pricey), and I saw a few complaints that the disc was extremely loose. This is not the case with the one I received. The disc that came with mine is actually a little stiffer than I would like, but it's not a big problem or anything. Maybe it will be looser when I inevitably have to buy new discs (which, by the way, is a huge perk; you don't have to buy a whole new stylus if the tip gets messed up, they sell replacement discs).I've only had this for a few days, so I don't know how far it goes in durability or lasting responsiveness, but hopefully it won't be a problem. From as much as I've played with it in the past few days, I highly recommend it to anyone who wants to write or draw on their device with much greater accuracy or detail.

Let me cut right the chase. I'm picky and very critical about electronic devices. I build my own PC's for gaming and occasionally do reviews for on pads, pc's and electrical gadgets.I have no bias for Android or OSX, Mac, Windows or anything else, I could care less and use both for what ever it is I'm working on. As I feel both have their benefits and downfalls.I Currently Have the ipad 2 & 3 ( 2 purchased, 3 was gifted). So why did I purchase this? I wanted a pad to take with me to work frankly And the ipads had limitations, particularly being that they didn't have USB or microSD, plus the Ipad 3 is noticeably heavier than this and makes holding it for long periods of time a workout on its own..and also at a price of 600 bucks, I didn't want to risk damage or theft. It's the cheapest pad and at nearly 300 bucks, I initially regretted my order, but figured If I didn't like it or it stopped working I can return.After much research, I opted on purchasing the Studio rather than going for a Samsung or Toshiba. Because after all, on paper the STUDIO 10 should perform as good as the Toshiba Thrive and possibly be on the same level as other "name brand" 2 processor tablets. I was honestly going to purchase a thrive.First Impression physical;I don't know why Idolian doesn't have pictures that do the pad justice. But they don't.Compared to Ipad 2, It felt somewhat lighter, definitely thinner and more streamlined. But the View on Idolian was better because the screen was a bit bigger minus the large space between were the screen ends and the side of the products as in the Ipad.Compared to ipad 2, it was brighter and offered sharper pictures, BUT not not sharp as the ipad 3. Same brightness and the difference in picture quality isn't really noticiable. Very easy to hold and use. I found it easier to hold and use than any of the iPadsThe Idolian is made from good material. I was expecting some rattling, or cheap parts. But you don't get that. Instead, you get a pad that has tight fit sliver back cover with openings for speaker that is covered by a nice steal mesh underneath two slits.Its got a volume rocker made of plastic on the same side as the camera. Its not loose and exactly to the slit size and slightly elevated from the side. Feels rock solid. My only critique is it would have been better if they used a different color rocker made of metal.On the bottom its got head phone, usb, HDMI, and Micro SD slots. All of which work and no cheap corner cutting.Now the camera. The biggest shocker is that the camera seems to take clear pictures! Now were not talking about iPad clear, but were talking about as good as a 5mp camera ( somehow the cam despite it being only 2 ( front as well) takes really good and clear pictures. Meaning There not blurry or terrible.Operating system ( where they really shine above competition)Screen:Unbelievably bright, clear and sharp colors. Despite it not being a glass. It was the same brightness as my ipad 3. Vibrant color and super sensitive screen. Responds From all corners and everywhere you touch it. How do you know? Place your finger on the very edge of the screen and flip to the next tab. It worked from all corners.When tablet is held at odd angles, there are no glares and is visible at eye level 180 degreeThe pad is lighting fast!! The beauty of Jelly bean on this particular model, is that they've given you the user moderator option. And you can pretty much custom everything.Go to the settings, then developer option and your in the heart of what makes this pad different that others.  Naturally I cranked up Windows animation, transition animations, and adjusted GPU settings and let this bad boy run. Pad took everything I threw at it with out chocking up! however, because you have everything cranked up, it means that it's going to use a lot of theatrics in transitioning and that annoys because I'm pretty impatient when it comes to electronics. I want them to respond fast. But I never felt it freezing or stalling.When the extra theatrics are turned off, I transitioned from window to window, apps to gadgets and basically everything so fast that my eyes had a hard time adjusting to the new screen and comprehending! For example on the iPad, there's a slight delay and some theatrics, but with this. When there's no theatrics, its FASTER THAN WHAT THE EYES CAN SEE AND MIND COMPREHEND.It comes equipped with flash player and by simply going to 1- settings 2-Developer options 3- Scroll down to Apps and unchecked the Google Application ( it being checked hides everything from google).The Play store appeared, and google search appeared. BTW this pad also has VOICE COMMAND! that actually works! and talks backs back. Youtube, Netflix, office products, adobe reader all work great.Home screen has the little  rectangular menu bar with with the icon to adjust brightness, wifi, Bluetooth and download. On the top left is the Google shortcut, but here's the best part. It's not obstructing your view, its in a small font, that clear white and next it is the microphone shortcut.On the right hand corner is the icon for menu tab(?). Through their you can access APPS AND WIDGETS. the cool part about Apps is that they exhibit a nice smooth 3D motion. Then you can check widgets and you the Pad transition from screen to screen super fast and while offering 3D motions. And if you crank up the  graphics, you can transition using fading in and out while zooming in and out and having the screen come up from behind slowly while the screen in the front slowly fades away.On the Menu bar, it gives you the option to see history tabs and actually erase them as well! you just slide it out and the box underneath moves up! You can also adjust volume, return to home screen or Return to previous screen.The device doesn't heat up as hot as other devices. And when you lower the brightness, I've gotten about 8-10 hours of usage. Videos brings the numbers down to 6-8.It's priced exactly at where it should be priced imo. And frankly speaking. I offers a lot with out the cheap feel and makes it very functional.If I notice anything else I'll post updates.

I purchased a regular sized bottle of this at a local store. It's the best fast dry top coat I have EVER used and I refuse to be without it anymore. It's simply incredible. My nails are do-your-hair dry in less than 90 seconds. I tried this, and I tried Out the Door. OTD would sometimes leave weird cloudy spots over dark colors, and I really didn't like that, and it did not dry as fast. Seche Vite is fast, crystal clear, leaves nails with a wet gloss look, and did I mention FAST? I always use with the Seche Clear basecoat, it seems to work best that way. It is a top coat that needs to be "floated" on to wet polish. I don't know a better way to explain it, but you need to have a heavier bead of it on the brush than you would normally use, this will avoid streaking the polish underneath. Don't wait for the polish to dry either. I find that as long as I apply while polish is wet, and float on a good thick coat of this, I don't have any streaking or pulling, and the polish doesn't transfer to the brush/bottle. It DOES tend to thicken over time, this can be fixed with a few drops of Seche Restore. Purchasing this size saved me a fortune, as I paid just a couple of dollars more than the price of the small bottle at the store, and got the equivalent of 9 bottles.

I didn't know what a standard seat post was and this would not mount without a new seatpost for me . This mounts on a 7/8 inch shaft end off whatever size seat post diameter your bike frame accepts . That said I now purchased the proper seat post and can review the seat itself . My bike came with a very expensive seat that was 100% quality leather . Only problem it was absolutely not comfortable . I am 55 , 6 1 and 195 pounds and need some cushion in my seat . This has two large springs that provide that . It has a very nice cover and looks very nice . I think it looks much better then the $80 seat that came off . It is very comfortable and the bumps are tempered by the springs and its cushion . It is also large enough that if you get a little sore you can move around on it easily . Further it has a place for your tailbone pocketed in it too that helps . It was definitely worth buying . Seats are easy to change as long as you understand what your frame requires for the seat post size and you have the proper mounting type at the top of the seat post . My original bike had a rail seat mount not a 7/8 round post like this seat requires so take a look and make sure you buy everything you need so you can get comfortable as soon as possible . This was a very nice addition to my bike and truly it looks very nice and of the highest quality . Few if anyone will know you bought a $25 seat thinking it is much more expensive . It will give your bike a very nice look the color scheme goes with any bike .There are two  large negatives for some individuals however . There are no tabs for mounting a tool kit or seat bag from the top of the bottom of the rear of the seat . Most cyclists need every possible option for carry options . This was especially critical as I bought a bike that offers less then normal opportunities already . Great seat - one star for no tool bag tabs and the second issue is it soaks up water when it is in rain leaving you with a wet seat , carry a plastic bag with your 99 cent rain coat . I wish I could tell you to put it in your seat bag.Lastly for those who need to go over how this mounts  remember your seat post lower diameter needs to be the proper size for your frame the top needs to be 7/8 inch to mount the seat too . This one worked for my 27.2 inch frame it is sold here so put it in search if you want to see what the top 7/8 diameter mount looks like . You just put this on the post and tighten the nuts with a wrench with the seat positioned level .Kalloy Straight Seatpost 27.2 x 300mm SilverNote : seat was great out of the box but is truly improving as it breaks in . I have started riding much and the more I use it the more comfortable it is getting . If you have a bad spine you should at least try it and if you can a firm or adjustable  suspension seat post also . The only downfall to using a suspension seat post is how it raises the minimum saddle height about 2 - 3 inches .

I love the Bose QC3s - they are one of my favorite things. I've owned them for over two years now, and traveled with them internationally. I use them at home, in Starbucks, even in libraries. But I value them the most when I'm on a plane. When I travel alone, I wear them from the white curb to my destination. I really love using them with or without music (the cord unplugs from the headset so you can wear them just for noise canceling benefit). True, they are ON EAR, as opposed to fitting AROUND your ear. I have not experienced the pain that some have talked about. I think it's more about attitude and practice, rather than a flaw in design. In other words, most things you wear for an extended period of time need some kind of adjusting. So occasionally I shift them slightly on my ears, rub my ears for a moment with my hand and then keep listening. No big deal! I like that they are more compact than other noise canceling headphones. I think the QC3 case is big and bulky, so I can imagine the other Bose models or the other brands are much larger. Regarding the battery, I've never run out of battery power - ever. If I lost or broke these, I would buy them again without doing any more research. I'm worried that the QC15s would be too hot and make my ears sweaty.As for sound I'm not that picky compared to other audiophiles. They sound awesome to me. They are Bose! Great bass and full dynamic range. But I care a little more about the size, use and noise canceling. I'm not looking for precise studio monitors, although I have owned those in the past.

Other reviewers covered many of the minute details such as weight, size, usage, durability, etc. so I do not want to repeat much of what has been said already. It is a heavy duty safe but no safe is going to do much good if you do not bolt it to the floor. One strong person can easily tote this safe quite a ways and 2 people can easily carry it out of your house and into their car.My safe did arrive with an oily film on it which wiped off easily in 5 minutes with a dry paper towel. The next thing I noticed was the dial; it does not move smoothly all the way around. It seems to catch through about 60% of the full turn which makes me a bit wary of it and the reason I did not give the safe 5 stars. I have 2 other Sentry safes, one which is much larger and one that is the same size but both of those have electronic locks. Funny side commentary here - regardless of which one you have, you cannot change the default code/combination on either the electronic or dial locks. In the event of a divorce, better be sure you have all the keys and no duplicates are around, else the STBX just might get ahold of some things you would rather they did not.One significant difference between the key lock on this safe over the other 2 safes is this one does not pop out when you unlock it.  Although this is a visual reminder it is unlocked, this safe does not have the feature for some reason. In comparison with the same sized safe I had already, this one seems to have a more robust seal and overall heft to the door. I assume this is because it is rated for 1 hr. rather than the half-hour for the other one and supposedly will safeguard digital media.Another reviewer commented that the instructions indicated you should store items in an airtight plastic bag and thought this seemed ludicrous. I can understand why for anyone who is not familiar with or had a fire-proof safe before; in fact, it is a really good idea to buy a dessicant pack and leave it in the safe as well, replacing as needed. The safe slowly emits moisture all the time, and even moreso when the temperature is warmer. Papers can come out feeling wet and mold can/will form on just about anything inside it for a prolonged period of time. The airtight plastic bag suggestion is to help protect your items from the moisture. Some may argue a dessicant pack defeats the purpose of the fire-proof portion, but a small 2-4 oz bag cannot absorb the amount of moisture released by the safe in a fire. It may reduce the effectiveness marginally, but it is better to protect your papers and other items from moisture damage. You can find these dessicant packs for under four bucks at different places.Overall, I like this safe and it will likely replace my other smaller one simply because of the higher fire rating and better overall seal.

I didn't expect enormously awesome things from this. It does allow you to get some interesting shots. However, there are a few things to know.First of all, if you plan to use this on any kind of track, it's going to be a pain. Mostly because getting the wheels perfectly straight is difficult. There ARE markings on this thing, but they're completely useless because of where they're located. Had they been longer and perhaps the axles more "boxy" it would have made it possible to line things up but - not really.Second, bearings in the wheels are very inconsistent. Some of mine are wobbly, Some of them are stiff, some of them are free spinning. This makes smooth tracking shots a little harder. You COULD upgrade or replace the bearings, but, by the time you do all that - probably should just invest in a slider unless you REALLY need a table dolly.This is a TABLE DOLLY which I think is the only thing it's good for. So if you have any other ideas - squash them despite what you see on youtube or kickstarter. I had some success with putting u-groove wheels on this for a slider type setup, but it left a lot to be desired even so.As for the table dolly thing, you're hit and miss here. Mostly because the table has to be 100% smooth even with camera stabilization and the bearings in this thing are well - Chinese.  So you may have to buy some new high quality bearings.I didn't expect much from this. It's small and it DOES have some interesting uses. So as long as you know the limitations, it's an OKAY product - especially when price is considered.However, if you're looking for cool shots to up your production values. I'd save a little more money and get a slider which can do 90% of what this could do and it'll do it better. Even if it's a budget slider.

The container for this shampoo says it is for "fine and flat" hair.  Well, I certainly fit that description.  I liked the shampoo - a thick gel which only required about a quarter coin size portion.  The first lathering did not completely remove all the styling and hair spray product residue in my hair so I lathered again, using slightly less that second wash time because my hair had started on the process of being clean.  The final rinse seemed to take a little longer to work all the product out of my hair than the shampoo I normally use, but eventually it was all rinsed out.  I've been using this shampoo for eight days now, plenty long enough to feel that I understand how it will work for me.  I do not notice any specific scent, just freshness.  As for giving my hair any "bounce", well, that just isn't happening.  For one thing, my hair is very short so not much room for bounce, but I'm also not noticing any lift for my hair.  I still have to use styling products in order to create any volume.  I tried to go without the styling product after the first shampoo with this Dove Oxygen Moisture Shampoo and it was not a pretty sight!I have dry scalp which can be quite itchy and this shampoo helps, maybe just a little bit, but not like a medicated shampoo will.  I think this shampoo will work best for me when I alternate it with the shampoo I normally use.  This one seems to be a good basic shampoo which will keep the itchies at bay for a day or so, but the "oxygen" feature isn't going to moisturize my scalp enough for me to totally abandon my regular medicated shampoo.  I wish it had worked because that medicated shampoo is really expensive!
